seo.title: Upholstery fabrics | Savery's of Broadway
seo.description: Designer upholstery fabrics chosen and hand applied in our Cotswolds workshop since 1942. Plains, checks, stripes and tickings for sofas and chairs to order.

hero.heading: Upholstery fabrics
hero.subtitle: Fabrics chosen for how they wear as much as how they look, cut and applied by hand in our Broadway workshop since 1942.
hero.imageAlt: Upholstery fabric samples displayed at the Savery's showroom in Broadway

howWeChoose.heading: How we choose fabric
paragraphs: Start with fibre. Wool, linen and cotton each behave differently on a sofa: wool resists crushing and hides marks in its texture, linen relaxes and softens with use, cotton takes colour evenly and adds strength in a blend. The fibre decides how a fabric feels in year five, not just in week one.

Then check durability. The Martindale rub count measures how a weave stands up to use: 20,000 rubs and above suits a family sofa, occasional chairs can sit lower, and hall benches and window seats that work hard should sit higher. We check the rating before we recommend anything, because a beautiful fabric with the wrong rub count is money wasted.

Match the pattern to the frame. A large repeat needs a generous three-seater where it has room to read; on a compact chair it looks cropped. Small checks and tickings do the opposite, bringing quiet interest to smaller pieces. We plan placement before cutting so the pattern runs unbroken across seat, back and arms.

Finally, look at the colour where it will live. North light cools a colour, lamplight warms it, and a shade that sings in the showroom can fall flat in a dark sitting room. Take samples home and look at them morning and evening before you commit. If you would like help narrowing it down, [book a consultation](https://shop.saverys.co.uk/pages/book-a-consultation) and we will work through it with you.
howWeChoose.imageAlt: Upholsterer working with fabric at the Savery's workshop in Broadway

plains.heading: Plains
plains.intro: Our plain upholstery range runs to fourteen colours, from pale neutrals through greens and blues to near-black. All are woven for everyday seating and available on any piece we make or recover.

patterns.heading: Checks, stripes and tickings
patterns.intro: Four woven designs, each in the same ten colourways. The shared palette means a check on the sofa, a stripe on a chair and a ticking on the cushions will sit together without effort.
patterns.note: Ask for them by design and colour: Audley check in Ivy, Darwin stripe in Twilight, Witley ticking in Linen.

naturalFibres.heading: Natural fibres
paragraphs: Most of what we upholster is wool, linen or cotton. Wool is the hardest wearing of the three and stays warm to the touch on a stone-floored Cotswold room. Linen softens with use and takes dye in muted, chalky tones. Cotton adds strength to blends and washes well in loose covers.

Natural fibres age rather than deteriorate, which is why we reach for them first. For the long read on how each one behaves and where it suits, see our [guide to natural upholstery fabrics](/journal/guide-to-natural-upholstery-fabrics).
naturalFibres.imageAlt: Natural upholstery fabric samples in the Savery's Broadway showroom

shop.heading: See the fabrics on furniture
shop.intro: Every fabric on this page can be ordered on a finished piece from our workshop. Browse the collections, or talk to us about a commission.

showrooms.heading: Visit a showroom
showrooms.body: The full fabric books can be viewed at our [Broadway](/showroom/broadway) and [Ludlow](/showroom/ludlow) showrooms. Handling a full-width sample tells you more than any screen, and we are happy to lend books for a few days.
